title: Fix requiring tests on Makefile changes
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Changes to how things are built shouldn't trigger the
'pr-should-include-tests` check.

#### How to verify it

A dummy Makefile change won't trigger the check.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

[Example failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4943480833703936)

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I’m not sure that “renovate” is exactly the differentiator — wasn’t the version that was failing _only_ the one with my additions?

That change was to the top-level `Makefile`; maybe adding _that_ to the `filtered_changes` filter would have allowed that test to pass, _and_ would avoid similar failures for any non-Renovate `Makefile` updates.
Case in point: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5273861176950784 fails the same way.
